# Implementation Plan: Random Geometric Obstacles

**Branch**: `003-obstacles` | **Date**: 2026-05-16 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/003-obstacles/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit-plan`. Companion artifacts: [research.md](./research.md), [data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/module-contracts.md](./contracts/module-contracts.md), [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md).

## Summary

Spawn random geometric obstacles in lanes ahead of the player, detect collision when an obstacle reaches the player's z, end the run on collision with a game-over DOM overlay showing the final score + time, and restart the run on the next input. Visual variety via at least five distinct 3D primitives.

**Approach**: A new pure-logic module `src/obstacles/` owns three concerns — a deterministic spawn generator (seeded RNG → next group's z + blocked-lane mask + shape variant), a collision predicate (`PlayerState` + `ObstacleGroup` → boolean), and the typed catalogue of shape variants. The runner-engine gets a fourth `RunState` value (`'game-over'`) plus two new transition functions (`endRun`, `restartRun`). The game-loop owns a small array of active `ObstacleGroup` records, advances their z each frame using the existing `distanceDelta`, drives the spawn generator when the world has scrolled past the next-spawn z, runs the collision predicate on the group nearest to the player, transitions to game-over on collision, and consumes the next input to restart. The renderer adds an obstacle-mesh factory keyed by variant id, pre-allocates a pool of meshes (no per-frame alloc), and positions them from the game-loop's group list. A new `<div id="game-over-overlay">` follows the existing overlay CSS pattern.

This slice introduces the **first fail state** in the game. The constitution remains valid; the `'game-over'` extension is genuinely required by FR-010 / FR-012, not speculative.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (`strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`) - inherited from 001 unchanged.

**Primary Dependencies**: No new dependencies. Existing: `three`, `vite`, `typescript`, `vitest`, `eslint`, `typescript-eslint`, `@types/three`, `@types/node`, `jsdom`.

**Storage**: None for this slice (no persistence per spec; FR-014 explicitly forbids cross-run state).

**Testing**: Vitest in `node` environment for the four new test files. No DOM/canvas needed - all new game logic is pure.

**Target Platform**: Same as previous slices.

**Project Type**: Single project - static web app.

**Performance Goals**: Same 60 FPS desktop / >= 30 FPS mobile budget. New per-frame work:
- Obstacle spawn check: O(1) integer comparison.
- Obstacle scroll: O(active groups) ~10 in a typical viewport.
- Collision check: O(1) - only the nearest unpassed group is tested.
- Renderer mesh positioning: O(active groups) cheap mesh.position writes.

Total added per-frame cost: tens of microseconds. No measurable perf impact.

**Constraints**:
- Critical JS budget: <= 500 KB gzipped (current 134.41 KB; new module adds ~5-8 KB).
- Boundary: `src/obstacles/` is pure logic; MUST NOT import `three` or DOM types. Enforced by the existing `no-restricted-imports` rule (the rule's allowlist for `three` is `src/renderer/` + `src/game/` only).
- The new `'game-over'` state MUST coexist with the existing `'pre-run' | 'running' | 'paused'` without breaking the score / timer derivations from 002 (which short-circuit when `tickMs` isn't advancing - already correct for game-over since `tickWorld` already gates on `runState === 'running'`).

**Scale/Scope**: ~250 LOC of new logic across `src/obstacles/` + ~80 LOC extension to `src/runner-engine/` + ~120 LOC extension to `src/game/game-loop.ts` + ~150 LOC extension to `src/renderer/three-renderer.ts` for the obstacle mesh pool + ~30 lines DOM/CSS for the game-over overlay. Tests add ~250 LOC. Total: ~880 LOC added.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

Evaluated against `.specify/memory/constitution.md` v1.0.0.

### Principle I - Simplicity & YAGNI

- [x] **No new dependencies.** Pure-logic module + renderer extension + DOM overlay - all using already-installed tools.
- [x] **The new `'game-over'` RunState value is required, not speculative.** FR-010 and FR-012 mandate the state machine extension; without it there is no place for "run is ended but we're not paused" to live.
- [x] **No premature abstraction in the spawn logic.** A single `nextObstacleGroup(prevZ, rng)` function returns the next group. No event bus, no interface for "spawn strategies".
- [x] **The obstacle catalogue is a flat const map**, not a class hierarchy. Each variant is `{ id, laneCount, ... }`. New variants are added by appending an entry.
- [x] **Restart is a state-machine transition, not a "scene reset" abstraction.** `restartRun(world)` returns a fresh `WorldState`; the game-loop reassigns its locals; the renderer mesh pool resets its visibility flags. No "Lifecycle" or "Scene" interface to maintain.

### Principle II - Test-First Discipline

- [x] **All new pure-logic functions have unit tests written before implementation.** Four test files declared upfront:
  - `src/obstacles/obstacle-spawn.test.ts` (spawn generator determinism, min/max gap, lane-mask shape constraints, two-lane adjacency).
  - `src/obstacles/obstacle-collision.test.ts` (player-in-blocked-lane true, player-in-passable-lane false, animation 50% threshold, boundary conditions).
  - `src/runner-engine/runner-engine.test.ts` extended: `endRun` transitions, `restartRun` zeroes counters, tickWorld no-op while game-over.
  - `tests/integration/lane-switch-flow.test.ts` extended: obstacle reaches player in blocked lane → `'game-over'`; restart input → `'running'` with `tickMs === 0`.
- [x] **Renderer obstacle mesh pool exempt from strict TDD** (visual code, per Constitution II) but its size + variant-id mapping is asserted via the contracts in `module-contracts.md`.

### Principle III - Library-First / Modular Design

- [x] **`src/obstacles/` is a new self-contained module** with a single public entrypoint (`src/obstacles/index.ts`). Imports only from `src/shared/types.ts` for `Lane` etc. - no Three.js, no DOM.
- [x] **Catalogue + spawn + collision live in one folder** (per constitution: subsystems are folders). They share types and constants but have distinct public entry points re-exported from `index.ts`.
- [x] **The renderer is the ONLY module that touches Three.js meshes for obstacles**; `obstacles/` returns variant identifiers and lane masks; the renderer maps id → mesh.
- [x] **ESLint `no-restricted-imports` rule already covers `src/obstacles/`** by default (it's not in the allowlist for `three` imports, so any accidental three import fails CI). No rule change needed.
- [x] **The game-over overlay DOM element is wired through `GameLoopHostElements` like the others** (no special handling). Adds one field; consistent with the score/timer additions in 002.

### Principle IV - Observability & Debuggability

- [x] **State transitions emit structured `console.debug` events.** New events: `obstacle_spawned` (with z + lane mask + variant), `collision_detected` (with player lane + obstacle lane mask), `run_ended` (with final score + timer), `run_restarted` (with seed for the new RNG stream).
- [x] **The existing debug overlay (`?debug=1`) already shows runState + tickMs + score + timer.** The new game-over state surfaces automatically; the score and timer naturally freeze at their final values.
- [x] **Errors include context.** None of the new functions throw; nothing to log otherwise.

### Additional Constraints (Platform / Performance / Accessibility)

- [x] Static web app, no backend.
- [x] Game-over overlay uses `position: fixed`, `pointer-events: none` on the backing div with `pointer-events: auto` only on the visible content (so non-content taps still pass through to the canvas-mounted game-loop input listener that consumes the restart) - matches the existing pause overlay pattern.
- [x] Overlay typography matches existing overlays (system-ui font family).
- [x] **Difficulty colour vs label constraint** (from constitution Additional Constraints): obstacles in this slice are NOT colour-coded by difficulty. The constraint applies when difficulty colour is the sole signal; here every obstacle is the same difficulty. The constraint re-activates when the green/orange/red question gates land in a later slice.

**Result**: ✅ All gates pass on initial check. No Complexity Tracking entries required.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/003-obstacles/
├── plan.md                 # This file
├── spec.md                 # Feature spec
├── research.md             # Phase 0 - decisions specific to this slice
├── data-model.md           # Phase 1 - entities and state transitions
├── contracts/
│   └── module-contracts.md # Phase 1 - new module API + DOM overlay contract
├── quickstart.md           # Phase 1 - slice validation steps
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md     # Spec quality checklist (passing)
└── tasks.md                # Phase 2 output (NOT created by /speckit-plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

This slice adds `src/obstacles/`, extends `src/runner-engine/` + `src/game/game-loop.ts` + `src/renderer/three-renderer.ts`, and adds the game-over DOM overlay to `index.html`. Everything from 001 + 002 stays where it was.

```text
geometry-dash/
├── index.html                       # gains <div id="game-over-overlay"> + matching CSS
├── src/
│   ├── obstacles/                   # NEW pure-logic module
│   │   ├── index.ts                 # re-exports catalogue, spawn generator, collision predicate
│   │   ├── obstacle-catalogue.ts    # const map of variant id -> { laneCount, ... }
│   │   ├── obstacle-spawn.ts        # nextObstacleGroup(prevZ, rng): ObstacleGroup
│   │   ├── obstacle-spawn.test.ts   # determinism, gap bounds, mask shape, adjacency
│   │   ├── obstacle-collision.ts    # collidesAt(player, group): boolean
│   │   └── obstacle-collision.test.ts
│   ├── runner-engine/               # EXTENDED: new game-over state + endRun + restartRun
│   │   ├── runner-engine.ts         # gains 'game-over' case + endRun + restartRun
│   │   └── runner-engine.test.ts    # gains end/restart test cases
│   ├── game/
│   │   └── game-loop.ts             # gains obstacle spawn/scroll/collide/restart wiring
│   ├── renderer/
│   │   └── three-renderer.ts        # gains obstacle mesh pool + per-variant geometry
│   ├── score/                       # UNCHANGED - still derives from tickMs, which freezes naturally
│   ├── shared/
│   │   └── types.ts                 # extends RunState with 'game-over'
│   ├── input-adapter/               # UNCHANGED
│   └── lane-state/                  # UNCHANGED
├── src/main.ts                      # resolves #game-over-overlay element
└── tests/
    └── integration/
        └── lane-switch-flow.test.ts # gains an obstacle-hit-then-restart end-to-end case
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-project static web app, unchanged from 001. The `src/obstacles/` module sits alongside the other pure-logic modules with the same boundary contract (no Three.js, no DOM).

## Phase 0 - Outline & Research

See [research.md](./research.md). Decisions specific to this slice:

- **Seeded RNG for spawn determinism**: a tiny xoroshiro / mulberry32 inline; deterministic given a seed, so tests are repeatable.
- **`'game-over'` over an alternative "is-dead" boolean**: a fourth `RunState` value is more consistent with the existing pattern (`'pre-run' | 'running' | 'paused'`) and gives a single state-machine transition table to reason about.
- **Effective-lane rule at animProgress = 0.5**: matches the assumption in spec.md; documented separately so it can be flipped without a constitution change.
- **Obstacle pool size**: 12 meshes per variant - decision recorded with the perf-vs-flexibility trade-off.
- **Restart input consumption**: same pattern as the pause-resume input (event consumed; does not also drive a lane change).

## Phase 1 - Design & Contracts

See [data-model.md](./data-model.md) and [contracts/module-contracts.md](./contracts/module-contracts.md).

- **Entities**: `ObstacleGroup`, `ObstacleVariant`, `ObstacleSpawnSchedule`. State extensions in `WorldState`: `'game-over'`.
- **Module contract**: `src/obstacles/` exposes three pure functions + the catalogue constant. `src/runner-engine/` gains two new functions. `src/game/game-loop.ts` gains one new wiring slot in `GameLoopHostElements` (the game-over overlay element). `src/renderer/three-renderer.ts` gains a public `updateObstacles(groups: ObstacleGroup[])` method.
- **DOM contract**: `<div id="game-over-overlay">` with three children: final score, final time, restart prompt.
- **Quickstart**: validation flow including dodge-and-die test, restart test, and the visual-variety test.

## Post-Design Constitution Re-Check

*Re-evaluated after Phase 1.*

- [x] Module boundaries in `contracts/module-contracts.md` keep `src/obstacles/` framework-free; Three.js stays confined to `renderer/` + `game/`. **Principle III holds.**
- [x] Data model adds one `RunState` value plus three pure entities. No new statefulness beyond the spawn schedule (which IS state but is local to the game-loop, owned and reset by it). **Principle I holds.**
- [x] Test obligations declared upfront for every new pure function. **Principle II holds.**
- [x] Structured `console.debug` events for the four new state transitions. **Principle IV holds.**

**Result**: ✅ Post-design re-check passes. Plan ready for `/speckit-tasks`.
